## [15:19 - 18:22]

best time to boost a post unless you're asking about actual time of day in which I don't think that matters too much either. But as far as timeline after the post is published, you can turn that post into an ad whenever you get around to it. And we typically change the ads, like I said, about three times a week because we post so often. We post so many new blog posts. But when we just had one a week, we had one Cindy Joseph live a week, we would turn that post into an ad and let that run for the full week until we had another blog post to run. And then I would go ahead and change it out at that point. All right. Two, three, four. Okay. So last is Laura. And she has a few questions about Boon Ladies Night. Awesome. How do you identify and connect with your guest? How do you know they are the right fit? So a lot of our Boon Ladies Nights are our ambassadors who we've already had conversations with. They've already sent us videos. We already kind of know them and know if they have resonated with our audience. So typically, when we pick an ambassador to join us on Ladies Night, she has performed, her blog has performed really, really well. She might be engaging in our Facebook posts or her own Facebook posts with our audience. So we already know, we know she likes our product, we know she likes our values and our pro-age revolution stance. So we already kind of know they are the right fit before we ask them to be on Boon Ladies Night with us. So typically, they are ambassadors already. We have brought people on who are, we have our women on who are not ambassadors, but Colleen, our COO, knew them really well and we knew they'd be a fit. So it's kind of more of a personal relationship that we build before we ask them to be on Ladies Night so we know that they're the right fit for us and for our audience. Do you prep them in advance? We typically do have a pre-Ladies Night call the night before where we look at their video, we talk about anything in the background that we ask them to move, make sure lighting is okay, make sure their sound is okay, ask them what they're using as far as video or audio goes, if we need to change anything. And we talked to them a little bit about a run-of-show. So our copy team will write a run-of-show for Colleen to go over as the night progresses. So we know what we want to talk about. We know based on other Ladies Nights that they like a demo. The women who are watching like it when we actually talk about the product and we didn't think that was a thing at first. We thought we were going to talk about everything else. We're going to talk about silver hair and menopause and wrinkles and all these other great topics. But we noticed people in the comments were starting to say, let's talk about the makeup. So we've changed our run-of-show as Ladies Night has progressed based on the comments that come in and what people want to see. So we do prep them in advance. We tell them that we are going to introduce them. We ask them how

## [18:22 - 21:23]

to pronounce their name correctly if that is an issue for us. We tell them what we're going to talk about. We tell them what products to have on hand if they have a boom trio, if we're talking about boom bright, if we're talking about our holiday sale. So we do prep them before we hop on the call definitely. We do not pay them to participate. No. If we want to talk about a specific product, there are times where we will mail them out the product ahead of time. We do send them thank you gifts, but there is no monetary exchange with them to participate in Ladies Night. And how do we identify and select the topics to be discussed on the call? So typically that goes in with what's going on in the moment. Any promotions that we have going on. For example, August we claim is boom pro-age month. So the whole month of August is pro-age month. So when we did Ladies Night, in August we talked about pro-age month. If we are running a skincare campaign either during the Ladies Night or before or after, not before, I'm sorry after, we will talk about any skincare routines. And then we kind of take into account what the comments were and what the feedback was from our previous Ladies Nights to talk about to kind of come up with what our audience is going to be interested in overall. So we take the feedback from our audience, excuse me, and we're listening to them and we're talking to them about that and that is the topics that we decide to come up with on the call. All right. And then also I wanted to ask you about your Ambassador Program. After following the Ambassador Program course, I still have a few questions. Besides inviting past or repeat customers, can you recommend other tips to find good branded ambassadors? What would you recommend to a new brand? I really love using customers. If you are a new brand, if you have a few customers, I would really, really suggest using them first. People who know and love your brand and your mission and your values and you know that they like those things and then build a relationship with them. I think it's better to start with a few ambassadors. You don't have to make it this huge program off the bat. I think it's really, really good to start with a few ambassadors who know and like and love your product. As far as when we first started, we would reach out to anyone on social media who kind of fit our brand guidelines. But what we realized was that if they had already been customers, they knew the product a little better. They were easier to kind of get the video from and talk about the product in a way and our brand in a way that we wanted to put out there in the world as opposed to an influencer or someone like that who we found on social media who kind of felt more like they were acting, which they were

## [21:23 - 24:28]

because they maybe got our product for the first time or they were doing it for a type of monetary exchange or anything like that. So I do think that getting customers is the best way to do it. And even if you start small, I think that's perfectly fine. And then you kind of like let it grow as it goes. I understand that your community manager is Ekeel. There's a lot of the one-on-one coaching to make sure you get the right content from ambassadors. Besides sending a high quality beauty light, do you have any tips to help the videos feel authentic and are still high converting? Do you coach them on what they have to say and how they have to say it? Do you do anything about that? So when we mail them their boxes, and I believe the updated PDFs that we send them are actually in the ambassador program in the downloads, but when we send them their boxes, they get kind of a pamphlet and a breakdown of what each product is, what each product is made out of, things to say, kind of like a cheat sheet of what to say about the product and what not to say about the product. And then we will go ahead and edit the videos. If there's any claims in there that we can't make because of legal reasons or anything like that, we will edit the videos if we see fit. There's also a contract that we put in there and let them know that kind of we have the authority to edit as we see fit these videos that they send us as well. And then if they have any questions, they do have that one-on-one with Ekeel that they're able to email her and chat with her and give her any feedback. We also put in our contract that we may ask them for reshoots. So if they send us a video and we do think it might sound a little scripted or if we think, if she says something about our mascara that we really can't edit or maybe doesn't sound right, we do have the ability to go back and say, hey, we know that this is a great video, but we notice this, this, and this, if you could fix those things and rerecord for us. Now, some people don't do it and we kind of chalk that up as a loss and kind of move on from there, but a lot of our ambassadors who are invested at that point really, really excited to be part of the ambassador program and they will reshoot for us. But besides the beauty light, we ask them for like to sit in front of natural light if they can, to film during the day if they can, to show their own personality, to not sound kind of scripted. But like I said, I think we give you and let me know if we don't, but I'm pretty sure in the ambassador program you get everything that we send them, all of the material in the PDF that we send them to let them know what to say about the products and a kind of general overview of the brand. All right. How do you keep your ambassador engaged and involved after they deliver their video and image? Do you have a follow-up strategy? We do have a little bit of a follow-up strategy and one that we're trying to expand on right now because we do notice that a lot of them fall off. A lot of times if we ask them to be part of Ladies Night, they do kind of stay more engaged with us, which

## [24:28 - 27:33]

is really, really nice. Sorry. But so we send them a few times a year kind of a thank you gift. Just for being part of the ambassador program, we created these pro-age bags. We create swag specifically for our ambassadors as well to keep them engaged, to keep them posting on social media for us. Do you have any advice for SEO or how to increase engagement on new blog post content? So I think as far as engagement on new blog post content goes, posting it everywhere, mailing it out to your email list, posting it on social media and as many different creatives as you can, as we talked about. I'm just putting it out there everywhere. I think it's the best way to get the traction on it and then running those page post engagement adds to it. Really following this iCOSA system, in my opinion, is the best way to get traction on those new blog posts. I'm not big on SEO. I think if you're writing your blog posts in a way that resonates with your audience, you're going to kind of naturally get some SEO. You're going to naturally get some keywords in there. But my understanding of SEO is not that deep either. So I'm also, I don't want to give you too much information on that. But I think just posting, when you have a new blog post, getting it out as far as you possibly can and as many different ways as you possibly can, is the best way to increase traffic to those posts. It's also the best way to increase traffic on previous posts, on old posts. So sometimes we'll go back and look at a post that did well and email that, repost it on social, reuse it all over the place as long as it's an updated blog post, if we need to do any updating to it.
